questions.question: The `bash builtins` -A option Each name is an associative array variable (see Arrays ).
questions.explanation: The `bash builtins` -A option Each name is an associative array variable (see Arrays ).

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -A option Each name refers to a shell function.
questions.explanation: The `bash builtins` -A option Each name is an associative array variable (see Arrays ).

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -f option Each name refers to a shell function.
questions.explanation: The `bash builtins` -f option Each name refers to a shell function.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -f option The variable is to be treated as an integer; arithmetic evaluation (see Shell Arithmetic ) is performed when the variable is assigned a value.
questions.explanation: The `bash builtins` -f option Each name refers to a shell function.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -i option The variable is to be treated as an integer; arithmetic evaluation (see Shell Arithmetic ) is performed when the variable is assigned a value.
questions.explanation: The `bash builtins` -i option The variable is to be treated as an integer; arithmetic evaluation (see Shell Arithmetic ) is performed when the variable is assigned a value.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -i option When the variable is assigned a value, all upper-case characters are converted to lower-case.
questions.explanation: The `bash builtins` -i option The variable is to be treated as an integer; arithmetic evaluation (see Shell Arithmetic ) is performed when the variable is assigned a value.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -l option When the variable is assigned a value, all upper-case characters are converted to lower-case.
questions.explanation: The `bash builtins` -l option When the variable is assigned a value, all upper-case characters are converted to lower-case.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -l option Give each name the nameref attribute, making it a name reference to another variable.
questions.explanation: The `bash builtins` -l option When the variable is assigned a value, all upper-case characters are converted to lower-case.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -n option Give each name the nameref attribute, making it a name reference to another variable.
questions.explanation: The `bash builtins` -n option Give each name the nameref attribute, making it a name reference to another variable.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -n option These names cannot then be assigned values by subsequent assignment statements or unset.
questions.explanation: The `bash builtins` -n option Give each name the nameref attribute, making it a name reference to another variable.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -r option These names cannot then be assigned values by subsequent assignment statements or unset.
questions.explanation: The `bash builtins` -r option These names cannot then be assigned values by subsequent assignment statements or unset.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -r option Give each name the trace attribute.
questions.explanation: The `bash builtins` -r option These names cannot then be assigned values by subsequent assignment statements or unset.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -t option Give each name the trace attribute.
questions.explanation: The `bash builtins` -t option Give each name the trace attribute.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -t option When the variable is assigned a value, all lower-case characters are converted to upper-case.
questions.explanation: The `bash builtins` -t option Give each name the trace attribute.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -u option When the variable is assigned a value, all lower-case characters are converted to upper-case.
questions.explanation: The `bash builtins` -u option When the variable is assigned a value, all lower-case characters are converted to upper-case.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -u option Mark each name for export to subsequent commands via the environment.
questions.explanation: The `bash builtins` -u option When the variable is assigned a value, all lower-case characters are converted to upper-case.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -x option Mark each name for export to subsequent commands via the environment.
questions.explanation: The `bash builtins` -x option Mark each name for export to subsequent commands via the environment.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -x option Display a short description of each pattern.
questions.explanation: The `bash builtins` -x option Mark each name for export to subsequent commands via the environment.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -d option Display a short description of each pattern.
questions.explanation: The `bash builtins` -d option Display a short description of each pattern.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -d option Display the description of each pattern in a manpage-like format.
questions.explanation: The `bash builtins` -d option Display a short description of each pattern.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -m option Display the description of each pattern in a manpage-like format.
questions.explanation: The `bash builtins` -m option Display the description of each pattern in a manpage-like format.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -m option Display only a short usage synopsis for each pattern.
questions.explanation: The `bash builtins` -m option Display the description of each pattern in a manpage-like format.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -s option Display only a short usage synopsis for each pattern.
questions.explanation: The `bash builtins` -s option Display only a short usage synopsis for each pattern.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -s option Use the first character of delim to terminate each input line, rather than newline.
questions.explanation: The `bash builtins` -s option Display only a short usage synopsis for each pattern.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -d option Use the first character of delim to terminate each input line, rather than newline.
questions.explanation: The `bash builtins` -d option Use the first character of delim to terminate each input line, rather than newline.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -d option If count is 0, copy all lines.
questions.explanation: The `bash builtins` -d option Use the first character of delim to terminate each input line, rather than newline.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -n option If count is 0, copy all lines.
questions.explanation: The `bash builtins` -n option If count is 0, copy all lines.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -n option Begin assigning to array at index origin.
questions.explanation: The `bash builtins` -n option If count is 0, copy all lines.

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -O option Begin assigning to array at index origin .
questions.explanation: The `bash builtins` -O option Begin assigning to array at index origin .

questions.question: The `bash builtins` -O option Discard the first count lines read.
